title: Information Bottleneck in Recurrence
concept: Forcing an entire variable-length sequence to compress into a single fixed hidden state creates an information bottleneck.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of the hidden state in a recurrent neural network?

options.text: To carry memory from past steps to the current step
To increase the speed of the CPU
To randomly scramble the input data

exercises.explain: A recurrent neural network uses its hidden state as a memory to carry information from previous steps to the current step.

cards.title: The Memory Limit
cards.body: Have you ever tried to memorize a long phone number while someone is shouting other numbers at you? Your brain has a limit on what it can hold at once. Recurrent neural networks face the exact same struggle!

cards.title: What is an Information Bottleneck?
cards.body: An information bottleneck happens when a neural network tries to squeeze a massive amount of data (like a long sentence) into a tiny, fixed-size container (like a single hidden state vector). Because the container size never grows, the network is forced to throw away important details.

cards.title: The Backpack Bottleneck
cards.body: Imagine trying to fit an entire library of books into a single backpack. If you only have one book, it fits easily. But if you try to pack 50 books, you have to throw away almost all of them to make them fit. The single backpack is a bottleneck that forces you to lose valuable stories.

cards.title: Squeezing a Paragraph
cards.body: If you feed a 100-word paragraph into a simple RNN, the hidden state vector at the final step is still only, say, 128 numbers. It is almost impossible for those 128 numbers to perfectly remember the exact words from the very beginning of the paragraph.

cards.title: Myth: Infinite Loops Mean Infinite Memory
cards.body: People often think that because RNNs loop over sequences, they can remember things forever. In reality, because the hidden state size is fixed, older information is constantly overwritten and faded out by new inputs.

cards.title: Squeezing vs. Looking Back
cards.body: Compare how information flows through a fixed bottleneck versus a system that can access all parts of the sequence.

left.text: Forces all past tokens into one small vector. Older details get squished and lost.

right.text: Allows looking back at any token directly, bypassing the single-vector bottleneck.

exercises.prompt: What structural feature of a standard RNN causes the information bottleneck?

options.text: The hidden state size stays fixed regardless of sequence length.
The sequence gets shorter as it goes.
The network runs out of training epochs.
The vocabulary size is too small.

exercises.explain: Because the hidden state has a fixed size, it cannot scale up as the input text grows longer. This fixed capacity acts as a bottleneck.

exercises.prompt: A fixed-size hidden state has a limited capacity to store information.
exercises.explain: True. The hidden state vector has a fixed dimension, meaning it has a hard limit on how much information it can carry.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing concept word.
exercises.sentence: Forcing a long sequence into a fixed-size vector creates an information ________.
exercises.explain: When we force a variable-length sequence into a fixed-size state, it creates an information bottleneck because the storage space does not grow with the input.

exercises.prompt: An AI says: "Our recurrent neural network will never lose details from long essays because its hidden state automatically grows larger to fit more words." Is this AI correct?

options.text: It depends on the activation function.

exercises.explain: The AI is incorrect because the hidden state has a fixed size and cannot scale up automatically to fit longer texts without losing information.

exercises.prompt: Why does a longer input sequence cause more information loss in a standard recurrent network with a fixed hidden state?

options.text: It runs out of computer memory (RAM) and crashes.
It is forced to overwrite and lose earlier information.
It automatically increases the size of its memory state.
It processes the text backward to double its memory.

exercises.explain: A fixed-size hidden state (the bottleneck) must store the representation of the entire sequence. As the sequence length increases, the model is forced to discard earlier information to make room for newer inputs, leading to data loss.
